name: Real Interest Rate HP-filtered

book_quotes.quote: Figure 10.8 plots the actual long bond real interest rate along with its HP-filtered value (parameter = 3), which is not "stable" over any meaningful time period. Indeed, it is highly volatile even over decades-a fact that flies in the face of the basic assumptions of standard theory about the rationality of agents and the efficiency of markets.
The filtered value, which is often taken to represent the long-run trend of a variable, falls from 10% in 1874 to -3% in 1917, rises to 8.3% by 1928, and falls again to -4.6% in 1946, and so on.

The long bond yield was created by splicing together two long series: the railroad bond yield from 1857 to 1937 was annualized monthly data on American bond yields taken from Macaulay (Macaulay 1938, Appendix Table 10, A142-A161) ... The US Producer Price Index (Wholesale Price Index) for 1857-1976 is from (Jastram 1977, 145-146, table 7) and updated thereafter from the BLS using growth rates of the PPI.

primary_source.table_or_series_id: Appendix 10.2 spreadsheet, iblongreal and iblongrealHP3 columns

formula: iblongreal_t = i_t - (PPI_t / PPI_{t-1} - 1) * 100; iblongrealHP3_t = HP_filter(iblongreal, lambda=3)

extension_candidates.subseries_label: Extension follows S1002 mechanics (FRED AAA - PPIACO inflation), then re-apply HP filter lambda=3
concerns: HP filter endpoint problem: adding new years changes trend at the splice point
lambda=3 is Shaikh's choice; not standard (Ravn-Uhlig 2002 suggests ~6.25 for annual)

methodology_notes: HP filter parameter lambda=3 is non-standard for annual data (Hodrick-Prescott originally suggested lambda=100 annual; Ravn-Uhlig 2002 suggested ~6.25). Shaikh uses 3 - to be flagged in Phase 4.
Series is a derivative of S1002, not a new data ingest.
Argument is rhetorical: the HP trend's volatility refutes Fisher's claim that the real interest rate is stable over time.
open_questions: Confirm Shaikh's specific HP implementation: one-sided vs two-sided filter, endpoint padding strategy.
Phase 4: should we report both lambda=3 and a more conventional lambda (6.25 or 100) for robustness?

review_history.notes: CD2 S054 ported; quotes verified p.467; HP lambda=3 flagged as non-standard
